anchors.label: Respond to every new inbound lead within 5 minutes
anchors.why: The Sales Playbook V2.0's single most-cited rule. 5-minute speed-to-lead is the largest conversion multiplier in the funnel — a response inside 5 minutes outperforms one at 30 minutes by a factor that dwarfs every other lever. Every minute past five is a slip.

anchors.label: Complete the 5-day follow-up cadence before any lead is abandoned
anchors.why: The playbook's 'Avoid This' explicitly forbids abandoning a lead before 5 days of follow-up. The cadence is the discipline that separates Comeketo from caterers who ghost after touch one. Every premature abandon is a trust withdrawal from the lead AND a training-data corruption for the whole sales engine.

anchors.label: Tasting → contract conversion rate, tracked per rep and per source
anchors.why: The fourfold exit (tasting → proposal → deposit → contract) is where the playbook's whole machinery lands or slips. Tasting conversion is the rollup KPI — everything upstream is judged by whether it produces tastings that convert. Per-rep and per-source breakdowns are where real operational learning happens; aggregated alone it's a vanity number.
anchors.source: MONITORING SHEET — 2026 Monthly Tasting Conversion Rate Summary

anchors.label: Admiration-first partner touches per week (no asks)
anchors.why: The partnership program's health is measured by admiration cadence, not referral volume. A week without a no-ask partner touch is a week where the ecosystem atrophies. The program's architecture assumes consistent low-pressure presence; silence reads as disinterest.

anchors.label: Partners referring Comeketo without being asked
anchors.why: The prime-directive outcome of the partnership program: 'We don't want to be the most recommended caterer. We want to be the safest caterer to recommend.' When a partner refers without being asked, the program is working as designed. This is the one metric where Rodbot is allowed mild animation — it compounds the trust arc.

anchors.label: Deposits cleared within contract-specified window
anchors.why: Cathlyn's surface. A deposit delayed past the contract window is a trust signal in both directions — the client may be hesitating, and the kitchen may be committing labor/ingredient inventory against a deal that hasn't yet anchored. Cadence fidelity here protects downstream operations.
anchors.source: Payments / Cash Collection / Cash Flow spreadsheet

anchors.label: No double-booking or over-commit against Bibi's kitchen capacity
anchors.why: Bibi owns kitchen bandwidth. Every commitment Rodbot helps draft (tasting date, event slot, menu scope) must respect the kitchen schedule as hard constraint — not aspiration. An overbooked kitchen is the one failure that Comeketo's 15-year reputation cannot absorb without visible cost.
anchors.source: Schedule of Catering Jobs Weekend + Weekly Prep List

regimes.description: Daily-fidelity streak regime for core partnerships (Rodrigo + Andre + ClickUp + Slack). Cheap to honor, load-bearing. Missing one is a real signal, not noise. Streak break rationale captured in TCL.

Network-tier regime for people outside the core partnerships. Daily anchor (one outreach) + weekly rollup (network breadth). Distinct from maintenance_fidelity because failure mode differs: partnership drops collapse specific deals; network drops collapse generalized not-alone-ness.
Medium-weight build regime for Twitter / community / LinkedIn / YouTube. Not daily-fidelity — weekly build progress is the unit. Anchor advances on surfaces being built, not on daily touches.

Direction regime for the red-team / certification work. High weight on 'is the work being hardened' dimension, low relevance to daily scoring. Scored on red-team passes completed, claims certified, open challenges surfaced.

Direction regime for Secretary's own capability growth (sub-agent fleet, orchestrator stages, voice model). Scored on shipped jurisdiction contracts, verified delegation round-trips, capabilities moved from spec to running code.

Low-weight maintenance regime for the stuff that silently breaks everything when it drops. AI billing today; eventually hosting, domains, backup integrity, local model weights — the substrate beneath the visible work. Daily glance is the honored action; missed is registered only when an alert eventually fires.

Capture-tier regime. Daily entries into the content journal that later feed public-surface work. Compounding is the mechanic: one entry a day is low-cost; ~365 entries a year is a reservoir. This regime accumulates more anchors over time (voice-memo capture, bookmark capture, etc.).

The maintenance tier for physical substrate: room (daily), laundry (weekly), and eventually whatever else belongs here (meals, sleep hygiene, exercise if declared). Low-weight individually, visible-as-pattern when multiple break in the same week.
Relational family tier, separated from physical maintenance and from partnership tier. Dad anchor today; extensible to other family relationships if declared. Daily touch is the unit.

notes: Anchors declared 2026-04-22 across two batches. Schema: { id, label, why, weight (0.0-1.0), comparator_hint, streak_worthy, cadence }.

Nine regimes across three functional tiers: relationship (maintenance + network + family), building (public-surface + framework-hardening + system-capability), and substrate (physical-maintenance + operational-substrate + reservoir-building).

Streak tracker reads streak_worthy flag. Ratio lattice will pairwise-rank when multiple anchors apply to one candidate move — most of the pairwise work lives across regimes, not within (cross-tier tradeoffs are where the real dominance questions show up: is one more Rodrigo text worth skipping room-cleaning tonight?).
